issues.description: extract_blocks reconstructed segments with split('\n')+join(), dropping one boundary newline at every segment boundary, so preamble_char_size + sum(block char_size) did not equal len(source_text); the projection was slightly low and not reliably comparable to guidance_report / the file length.
issues.resolution_notes: Rewrote extract_blocks to slice the ORIGINAL text by computed line char-offsets (offset += len(line)+1), so preamble + every block byte-exactly re-partition the file. Added test_extract_blocks_size_accounting_is_exact and a no-trailing-newline variant; both assert the sum invariant AND that concatenation reproduces the source. Re-ran the dogfood projection from the saved raw response: current_chars now == len(text) == 154780 (was 154711).

issues.description: A malformed merge (classification='merge' with no/invalid merge_target) was flagged as an error but still returned as a MERGE classification, and project_size counted every MERGE as 0 retained chars -> projected savings too optimistic.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: A merge without a valid target leaves the block unclassified -> retained conservatively.
issues.resolution_notes: R2: parse_triage_response rejects a MERGE with merge_target None or == index (records an error, drops it from the returned list so the block becomes unclassified -> retained). R2 follow-up (verifier found the out-of-range / fold-into-removed-block gap still open): project_size now computes the set of SURVIVING block indices (keep-active/promote) and counts a MERGE as removed ONLY when merge_target is in that set -- a None, out-of-range, or itself-removed target is retained. Tests: parse rejection (cls == []), test_parse_merge_to_self_is_rejected, test_parse_valid_merge_is_kept, test_project_size_merge_without_target_is_retained_defensively, and test_project_size_merge_into_nonsurviving_target_is_retained (out-of-range, fold-into-archived, fold-into-survivor cases).

issues.description: Reference-conflict guard did not fully match the 'referenced by active guidance' definition: project-guidance matching was raw substring (false-positive on id prefixes, e.g. L-064-1 inside L-064-12), and cross-lesson references were counted even when the referring block was itself being removed.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: Token-exact id match in project-guidance; only count references from surviving blocks.
issues.resolution_notes: Added _id_token_in (negative-lookbehind/lookahead whole-token match) for the project-guidance check; flag_referenced_archives now computes surviving block ids (keep-active/promote) and only treats a cross-lesson reference as a live conflict when its source survives (project-guidance.md always counts). Added test_flag_referenced_archive_ignores_reference_from_removed_block and test_reference_graph_token_match_avoids_id_prefix_false_positive.

issues.description: The cp1252/ASCII safeguard test used ASCII-only input, so it never exercised the real fold-to-ASCII display path.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: Use a non-ASCII title and assert the stdout fold is ASCII-safe while the UTF-8 report keeps fidelity.
issues.resolution_notes: Replaced with test_render_report_folds_real_non_ascii_titles_for_stdout (em-dash title: report preserves it, _to_ascii fold is pure ASCII) plus test_to_ascii_replaces_non_encodable.

issues.description: Recipe doc (Step 2) and the module docstring claimed raw routed output is persisted 'before any parse', but the shipped helper parses (exception-safely, no print) inside run_triage before main() writes --out.
issues.verificationMethod: gpt-5-4 cross-check of doc vs run_triage/main control flow.
issues.suggestedTestOrCheck: Narrow the claim to the guarantee the code provides (persist before console display).
issues.resolution_notes: Narrowed both the module docstring and docs/guidance-backlog-remediation.md Step 2 to 'persisted to UTF-8 before any console display'; noted parsing is exception-safe and does not print, and the on-disk copy is the durable record.
